pattern: Tallying how often each value appears and then asking whether those counts are all distinct reduces to comparing the size of a set of counts against the number of values.
hints: First reduce the array to how many times each value occurs.
The question is whether any two distinct values share the same occurrence count.
Build the frequency map, collect its counts into a set, and return true exactly when the set is as large as the number of distinct values.

edge_inputs.description: three values with distinct counts three, two, one
two distinct values each appearing once collide
many values all sharing count one

stress.description: random arrays over a small value range so frequency collisions are common
stress.note: narrow range makes both true and false outcomes appear
